Empires & Puzzles Serena Review: The Beachside Gossip Queen

Serena brings a new flavor of chaos to Empires & Puzzles with her 'Spreading Gossip' skill. She’s not just hitting targets; she’s infecting them with fiends that clone themselves and ruin accuracy. Is this socialite the anti-healing solution you need, or just another pretty face on the bench? We break down the stats, the strategy, and the best ways to use her.
Serena Review: Spreading Gossip, Fiends, and Missed Attacks
Serena, The Shoreside Socialite: Spreading Rumors, Fiends, and Missed Attacks
Serena is a 5-star Dark (Purple) hero from the

event, bringing a touch of high-society drama to the battlefield. Released in mid-2025, she serves as a unique hybrid: part sniper, part fiend-summoner, and full-time troublemaker. Her gimmick revolves around "gossip" that literally spreads from hero to hero, manifesting as fiends that block healing and blind enemies. While she doesn't hit with the raw force of a heavy nuker, her ability to disrupt the enemy team's sustain and accuracy makes her a strategic nuisance. Think of her as the person who starts a rumor at a party that eventually ruins everyone's night.

Serena’s design screams "beach vacation," complete with a stylish sun hat and a confident smirk that suggests she knows something you don't. The purple skin tone marks her as a Dark hero, but the vibrant beach attire and bright background make her stand out against the usually gloomy and armored Dark roster. It’s a refreshing change of pace—she looks like she’s about to sip a cocktail while your team slowly disintegrates from gossip.
Serena's stats present a mixed bag when viewed through the lens of the current meta (early 2026).
This is her standout stat. It is very competitive, matching or even exceeding many modern heroes who sit around the 2260 mark. She can take a hit.

Here is where she shows her age slightly. Modern damage dealers are pushing 1300+ Attack, and tanks are easily clearing 1300 Defense. Serena sits firmly in the "solid but not elite" tier. She relies on her special skill's utility rather than raw stats to make an impact.
"Spreading Gossip" is a skill that rewards patience and strategic targeting.

At Average speed, 400% damage to a single target is frankly underwhelming in today's game. It won't one-shot a healthy modern 5-star. Treat this as the "delivery system" for the fiend, not the main event.
This is the core of her kit.
It forces the enemy to waste healing resources.
This is massive. It applies to
attacks, including Special Skills. A 45% miss chance is effectively a 45% damage reduction from that hero until the fiend is removed.

The fiend clones itself to nearby enemies every turn. This is the killer mechanic. If the enemy cannot heal immediately, one fiend becomes three, then potentially five. A team covered in these fiends is a team that can't heal and can't hit.
Average. She needs 10 tiles to charge, or 9 with a modest amount of mana support (Level 23 Mana Troop or Level 11 Magic/Styx + Class/Costume bonus).

-45% accuracy is statistically stronger than a -45% attack debuff because a miss triggers zero secondary effects (no status ailments applied, no minions summoned, etc.).
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
family and her passives add durability and extra chip damage.
The family bonus reduces damage received from DoT (Damage over Time) effects. It’s situational but nice against burn/poison heavy teams.
Adding ~278 damage over 2 turns isn't huge, but it's free damage that bypasses defense. It helps soften up targets.
Useful specifically against other Beach or Atlantis water-based heroes, but generally niche.

allows Serena to punish enemies who target her. Given her high health pool, she can survive hits and reflect decent damage back. It forces the enemy to think twice before sniping her, potentially buying time for her fiends to spread.
Upon release, Serena was seen as an interesting "soft control" hero. The community was divided on her low initial hit damage versus the potential of the spreading blind.

While her HP holds up, her damage output has fallen behind the curve of the "Astral" and "Construct" heroes released recently. However, her utility is timeless. Blindness and Healing Block are mechanics that don't rely on stats to be effective.
She is not a top-tier defense hero anymore due to the prevalence of fast cleansers and heavy hitters. However, on
(where healing is disabled, making fiends permanent), she can be an absolute monster.
, which gives her a chance to resist status ailments. This is excellent for ensuring she fires her skill.
Maximizes her high HP and shores up her lower Defense. Keeps her alive longer to let the fiends spread.
Her 400% snipe becomes even weaker (essentially a wet noodle).
Makes the initial hit sting a bit more and slightly increases the water DoT (if based on attack).
She might die before the "gossip" spreads effectively.
path is the way to go. Serena is a support/disruptor, not a killer. Keep her alive.
A permanent (until healed) blind is one of the strongest debuffs in the game.
If ignored, the fiends multiply, overwhelming the enemy team without Serena needing to fire again.
Resistance to ailments makes her reliable.
400% damage to one target at Average speed is weak.
If the enemy has a strong healer ready to fire, the fiends are wiped instantly.
She doesn't win games quickly; she wins them slowly. In a fast meta, "slow" can mean "dead."
Unlike some fiends that bite every turn, these only absorb healing and blind. They don't actively kill the enemy.
With no healing allowed, her fiends (and the blind) are permanent. The enemy
Great against teams that rely on a single healer or passive healing.
Very fun for locking down bosses with blindness.
Layering Dodge with Serena's Blind means the enemy effectively has a near-zero chance of landing a clean hit.
Combine her healing absorption with heroes that reduce max HP or block healing completely to suffocate the enemy team.
Heroes who summon Mega Minions often replace or block fiends.
Healers who specifically target fiends (like Esme) will hard counter Serena.
Turns fiends into minions/health. Do NOT bring Serena against Arco.
If your team fills the enemy slots with minions, Serena's fiends have nowhere to land. Avoid pairing her with heavy minion spammers if you want to use her fiends.

If you pulled Serena early, congratulations! She will carry you through map stages. Bosses can't kill you if they can't hit you. Focus on keeping her alive with healers and defense buffers. Don't worry about her damage; let the fiends do the work.

Serena is a "luxury" toy. She's not essential for competitive wars, but she opens up a unique win condition against stall teams. If you have the spare Monk emblems, she's a fun project, especially for tournaments. However, don't prioritize her over modern meta-defining heroes.
The community reaction to Serena was... complicated.
"She was a mediocre healer in beta... They changed her to be a hitter."
— This shift confused some players who wanted a new Dark healer.
— A common sentiment reflecting that she wasn't seen as a "must-have" like the Christmas heroes of old.
"The multiplying fiends work magic against some of the good minions... I used mostly against C Diaochan."
— A savvy player noting her niche use against minion-heavy meta defenses.
"She's hot so that makes her level worthy."

Serena is the definition of a "fun" hero. She brings a mechanic—spreading gossip—that perfectly matches her theme and offers a unique gameplay loop. She isn't going to dominate the top 100 leaderboards, but in the right hands (and definitely in Bloody Battle), she can make the enemy team look like a clumsy, uncoordinated mess. Is she the best Dark hero? No. Is she the best at ruining a healer's day? She's definitely in the conversation.
One copy is plenty—level her, lock her, and unleash the gossip when the time is right.
A detailed review of Serena, the 5-star Dark hero from the Beach Party family. We analyze her stats, spreading fiends, accuracy debuff, and whether she's worth your summons.
